claim_bullets.bullet_text: In GC environments, arrays marked as no longer used are eventually recycled, meaning the garbage collector reclaims the memory they occupy (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.126-140).
Despite the maximum amount of memory not growing, creating short‑lived arrays causes thrashing, leading to extensive copying of elements and high overhead (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.126-140).

We may not be creating 3,000 stack frames, but we are creating three thousand new arrays and copying elements into each, which may contribute to the performance hit (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.126-140).

source_record_text: The chapter explains how JavaScript's array handling causes significant memory thrashing when using recursion, leading to garbage collection overhead and performance degradation.
